page: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring an Intel Xeon (Cascadelake) x86_64 processor with 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is configured with 12.0 GB of memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography.

Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1466.08 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.18 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is limited, as evidenced by a weak stress-ng multi-core score of 5747.52 and a poor multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 17.19 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small-block reads at 421.33 GB/sec, but drops to a weak 36.96 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

This server offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources or sustained multi-threaded performance. It performs well in key-value store environments, achieving 6.4 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, caching layers, and basic single-threaded processing, while being less optimal for database operations or intensive multi-threaded compute tasks.
description: The Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon (Cascadelake) x86_64 processor with 4 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It features 12.0 GB of RAM and 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1466.08 and strong Redis SET performance of 6.4 million operations per second. However, multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the weak to poor categories, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. This server represents a cost-efficient option for workloads with moderate resource demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, caching, and single-threaded processing tasks, rather than intensive database operations or multi-threaded compute workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel is a High Performance server with 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 12 GB RAM, and 260 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel features 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 12 GB RAM, and 260 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose and caching workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared Intel Xeon processors and fast NVMe storage for general-purpose and caching workloads.

12.0 GB system memory and 260 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core performance and 6.4 million Redis operations per second
Weak multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and general-purpose tasks
